metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: Practice comparing the initial and goal boards before writing anything. One value moves, but one value must stay where it is.

introduces: reading initial and goal states before planning a solution

messages.title: Read the target before touching the program
messages.body: This level is about planning first. Compare both boards and notice that not everything changes: one value must move, but another value on B must stay exactly where it already is.

messages.title: Not every visible value should move
messages.body: If B lost the value it already had, then the program changed more than the goal asks for. Match the target board carefully before running again.

messages.title: Solve the exact difference
messages.body: A good plan starts by asking what is different between the two boards. This level can be solved by changing only that exact difference.
